summary: Matched the common fighter TurnRun physics callback by replacing the placeholder for `ftCo_TurnRun_Phys` with C logic using fighter input, common dash/run acceleration attributes, terminal velocity, ground friction, and the shared TurnRun friction multiplier. The PR also added a `turnrun` motion-vars layout to `ftCommon_MotionVars` and named `ftCommonData.x60` as `x60_turnrunFrictionMul`.
what_changed: Implemented `ftCo_TurnRun_Phys(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` in `src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c` where it had previously been a decompilation placeholder.
Added includes for `ft/fighter.h` and `ft/ftcommon.h` so the implementation could access fighter/common declarations.
Computed TurnRun acceleration from `fp->input.lstick.x`, `co_attrs->dash_run_acceleration_a`, and signed `co_attrs->dash_run_acceleration_b`.

Used `ftCommon_8007C930` with `co_attrs->gr_friction * p_ftCommonData->x60_turnrunFrictionMul` when there is no max velocity or when the acceleration direction does not satisfy the TurnRun-specific condition.
When `fp->mv.co.turnrun.accel_mul * accel < 0`, adjusted acceleration toward `max_vel` with friction compensation and stored the result in `fp->xE4_ground_accel_1`.
Called `ftCommon_8007CB74(gobj)` at the end of the physics callback.
Added `mv.co.turnrun` to `union ftCommon_MotionVars` with padding through offset `fp+234C` and a named `float accel_mul` field.
smart_moves: The match relied on adding the correct action-specific motion-vars substructure instead of treating the offset as raw memory in the physics function.
The PR promoted a formerly unknown common-data field to a typed, usage-based name, making the friction calculation self-documenting.
The implementation mirrors game physics control flow with separate branches for zero target velocity, acceleration opposite the stored TurnRun multiplier, and fallback ground friction handling.
The code uses `ftCo_DatAttrs* co_attrs = &fp->co_attrs` to keep repeated fighter common-attribute accesses concise while still preserving explicit field names.
decomp_lessons: When matching common fighter physics callbacks, inspect both per-action motion variables and shared `ftCommonData`; matching often requires naming fields in headers, not just filling in a function body.
For action-specific motion vars, add a union member with padding to the known offset when only the relevant field is identified; here `turnrun.accel_mul` lives at `fp+234C` after 12 bytes of padding.
Use behavior-derived names for previously unknown constants when the function makes the purpose clear, as with `x60_turnrunFrictionMul` multiplying ground friction during TurnRun.
Stick-input-derived acceleration often combines an analog-scaled term with a signed constant term, and terminal velocity may also scale directly by stick X.
Physics matches can depend on preserving branch structure around sign checks and clamp-to-terminal-velocity adjustments rather than simplifying the math too aggressively.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_TurnRun_Phys` follows the common fighter action callback naming pattern: `ftCo_<Action>_<Callback>`.
The motion-vars union uses a lowercase action member name, `turnrun`, under `fp->mv.co`.
The newly identified motion-var field is named by role as `accel_mul`.
The common-data field at offset `+60` was renamed from `x60` to `x60_turnrunFrictionMul`, preserving the offset prefix while adding a semantic suffix.
Existing common fighter attribute names used in the match include `dash_run_acceleration_a`, `dash_run_acceleration_b`, `dash_run_terminal_velocity`, and `gr_friction`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced typed access to `fp->mv.co.turnrun.accel_mul` at `fp+234C` by defining padding in `ftCommon_MotionVars`.
Used a signed ternary addition for `dash_run_acceleration_b`: positive stick input adds it, otherwise subtracts it.
Kept explicit nested branches for positive and negative acceleration to clamp acceleration relative to `max_vel` after applying or removing friction.
Stored the computed acceleration into `fp->xE4_ground_accel_1` only in the branch where `fp->mv.co.turnrun.accel_mul * accel < 0`.
Preserved calls to existing helper functions `ftCommon_8007C930` and `ftCommon_8007CB74` rather than inlining their behavior.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: No substantive technical review feedback was present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other uses of `p_ftCommonData->x60_turnrunFrictionMul` to confirm whether the name is TurnRun-specific or used by related ground movement states.
Search for additional `mv.co.turnrun` fields around `fp+2340` to replace the padding in the `turnrun` motion-vars struct with named fields.
Compare `ftCo_TurnRun_Phys` with dash, run, walk, and turn physics callbacks for shared acceleration/friction patterns.
Investigate whether `fp->mv.co.turnrun.accel_mul` is initialized in the TurnRun animation/action-change code.
